variable_changed: precision=fp32 (vs control's bf16 autocast) -- Wave E bf16-vs-fp32 axis
verdict: NULL RESULT on quality (+0.0083 vs control, well within noise) -- bf16 autocast and fp32 reach the same loss. But fp32 is real and substantial on SPEED: ~296K tok/s vs control's ~455K tok/s (~35% slower) -- bf16 tensor-core throughput is free accuracy-wise at this scale, confirming D-009/CLAUDE.md's bf16-by-default choice.

variable_changed: gradient_checkpointing=true (vs control's false) -- Wave E memory/speed axis; loss should be identical (recompute, not an approximation)
verdict: NULL RESULT on quality (-0.0088 vs control, within noise) -- checkpointing recomputes exactly, doesn't approximate, so loss parity is expected and confirmed. Real cost: ~333K tok/s vs control's ~455K (~27% slower) at this seq_len/batch. Real payoff (see bench_activation_memory.py, not this run): ~1.72x peak memory reduction at every seq_len, letting seq_len=2048 fit at micro_batch=64 where the uncheckpointed model OOMs.

variable_changed: batch factorization mb=32,accum=4 (vs control's mb=64,accum=2; same effective batch=128 sequences/step) -- Wave E micro-batch/accum equivalence check
verdict: NULL RESULT on quality (+0.0008 vs control, within noise) -- same effective batch (128 seqs/step), different factorization, same loss trajectory as predicted. But real and substantial on SPEED: ~248K tok/s, the SLOWEST run in the wave (~45% slower than control) -- 2x the micro-steps of the control at half the size means 2x the Python-loop/kernel-launch overhead for the same FLOPs, confirming D-022's launch-overhead-bound finding.

variable_changed: batch factorization mb=128,accum=1 (vs control's mb=64,accum=2; same effective batch=128 sequences/step, zero grad accumulation) -- Wave E micro-batch/accum equivalence check
verdict: NULL RESULT on quality (+0.0040 vs control, within noise) -- same effective batch, zero grad accumulation, same loss as predicted. Real and the FASTEST run in the wave: ~525K tok/s (~15% faster than control) -- fewer, bigger micro-steps amortize launch overhead best. Combined with mb32_accum4: loss is invariant to batch/accum factorization but wall-clock is NOT -- always prefer the largest micro-batch that fits.

variable_changed: tie_embeddings=false (model_s_notie.yaml, +3.07M params vs control's tied 9.71M) -- Wave E weight-tying axis, empirical check of D-016's parameter-budget argument
verdict: REAL but caveated (-0.0278 vs control, just past the noise floor) -- untied is BETTER here, the opposite direction from a naive reading of D-016's parameter-budget argument. Important confound: this is NOT a param-matched comparison (12.79M vs control's 9.71M, +31.6% params) -- the win could just be extra capacity from the unshared unembedding matrix, not evidence that tying itself hurts quality. A fair test needs a param-matched untied variant (shrink d_model/layers to compensate). D-016's budget-efficiency argument (same quality at lower param cost) stands; this run does not overturn it, but does NOT confirm tying is quality-neutral at fixed layer shape either.

variable_changed: torch.compile=true (vs control's uncompiled) -- Wave E compile attempt; CUDA only, MPS is unreliable per CLAUDE.md so this config is meant for the gpuhub 5090
verdict: NULL RESULT on quality (+0.0014 vs control, within noise) -- torch.compile doesn't change what's computed, confirmed. Real, modest SPEED win: ~535K tok/s (~18% faster than control) on the RTX 5090/CUDA -- compile attempt succeeded cleanly (no fallback/graph-break issues at this model size), unlike CLAUDE.md's MPS-unreliable warning which doesn't apply here since this ran on CUDA. Worth defaulting to compile=true for future CUDA runs at this scale; MPS remains untested/not recommended per CLAUDE.md.

variable_changed: dense FFN -> DeepSeekMoE (8 routed + 1 shared, top-2, active-param-matched), aux_loss balancing -- Wave F flagship, half of the aux-loss-vs-aux-loss-free pair
verdict: REAL win vs dense control (-0.0907, >4x noise floor) -- DeepSeekMoE reproduces at S-tier: more total capacity (18.61M vs 9.71M) at matched active params/token. See D-044 for a real val_loss measurement bug caught and fixed before this verdict was written.

variable_changed: dense FFN -> DeepSeekMoE (8 routed + 1 shared, top-2, active-param-matched), bias_free (DeepSeek-V3) balancing -- Wave F flagship, half of the aux-loss-vs-aux-loss-free pair
verdict: REAL win vs dense control (-0.0828), statistically tied with aux_loss balancing (0.0079 apart, within noise) on FINAL quality -- but balances measurably SLOWER (std/mean over experts stays >0.07 through step 600 vs aux_loss reaching that by step ~200), the real DeepSeek-V3 tradeoff: no gradient interference, but a bounded per-step correction.

variable_changed: +1 sequential MTP head predicting t+2 (loss_weight=0.3), teacher-forced, shared final_norm+lm_head -- Wave F flagship, does denser per-token supervision help or hurt at this fixed active compute?
verdict: NOT distinguishable from noise (+0.0167 vs control, at the edge of the 0.015-0.02 floor) -- the extra head demonstrably learns its own (harder) t+2 task (loss 9.71->3.78) but does not measurably help or hurt the main next-token objective at this scale/budget.

variable_changed: domain_share=0.0 (control, general books+dictionary only) -- Wave G domain-mix ablation
verdict: Control (0% domain share). val_loss=3.9800 -- baseline point for the domain-mix dose-response curve (D-045). Budget note: this wave uses a reduced 49.15M-token budget (D-045), not the usual ~98.3M, so NOT directly comparable to other waves' val_loss numbers -- only compare within this ablation.

variable_changed: domain_share=0.1 (finance/self-help/wisdom mixed into training stream at 10%) -- Wave G domain-mix ablation
verdict: REAL, monotonic degradation of GENERAL val loss as domain share rises: +0.0353 vs control (D-045). At only 10% share the finance/self-help register is already diluting the general (philosophy/dictionary) val distribution.

variable_changed: domain_share=0.25 (finance/self-help/wisdom mixed into training stream at 25%) -- Wave G domain-mix ablation
verdict: +0.0749 vs control (~4x the D-035 noise floor, though that floor was measured at a different budget) -- continues the monotonic trend. Samples already show finance/economics-inflected vocabulary ("Export of the State", trade/currency terms) at just 49M tokens.

variable_changed: domain_share=0.5 (finance/self-help/wisdom mixed into training stream at 50%) -- Wave G domain-mix ablation
verdict: +0.1642 vs control, the largest gap in the sweep (>8x the D-035 floor) -- confirms the expected specialization/generality tradeoff: mixing in enough domain data to matter necessarily costs general val loss. See D-045 for the full dose-response verdict and the phase-9 recommendation.

variable_changed: n_epochs=1 over books-only pool -- Wave G multi-epoch overfitting lab
verdict: 1 epoch over the 14.14M-token books-only pool (undertrained, D-045) -- train/val gap +0.268, both still high (val=5.695). Reference point for the overfitting-lab's gap-vs-epochs curve.

variable_changed: n_epochs=4 over books-only pool -- Wave G multi-epoch overfitting lab
verdict: 4 epochs over the books-only pool -- train/val gap widens to +0.344 (train=4.104, val=4.448). Val loss is already close to its eventual plateau (~4.1-4.45) even this early.

variable_changed: n_epochs=16 over books-only pool -- Wave G multi-epoch overfitting lab
verdict: 16 epochs over the books-only pool -- gap widens sharply to +0.921 (train=3.207 keeps falling, val=4.128 plateaus/flattens) -- textbook overfitting signature: train keeps memorizing the small repeated pool while val stops improving. Confirms the phase-5 spec's predicted 'gap opens' pattern cleanly (D-045).

variable_changed: n_params=5.00M (d_model=128,n_layers=15) @ fixed 200M tokens -- Wave G mini scaling law
verdict: Mini scaling law point 1/4 (5.00M params). Still monotonically improving at step 3050 (no repetition-driven overfitting yet at this budget/capacity) -- val_loss=3.4031 is both the best and final value. See D-045 for the full fit.

variable_changed: n_params=9.71M (d_model=192,n_layers=15, reused model_s.yaml) @ fixed 200M tokens -- Wave G mini scaling law
verdict: Mini scaling law point 2/4 (9.71M params, reused model_s.yaml). Still monotonically improving at step 3050, val_loss=3.2663 best=final. See D-045.

variable_changed: n_params=24.79M (d_model=320,n_layers=16) @ fixed 200M tokens -- Wave G mini scaling law
verdict: Mini scaling law point 3/4 (24.79M params). Starts overfitting the repeated 17.66M-token pool before the 200M-token budget ends -- best val_loss=3.1655 @ step 2400 (157M tok), final degrades to 3.1789 (+0.0134). Fit uses the BEST value. See D-045.

variable_changed: n_params=50.40M (d_model=384,n_layers=25) @ fixed 200M tokens -- Wave G mini scaling law
verdict: Mini scaling law point 4/4 (50.40M params). Overfits MUCH earlier and more severely -- best val_loss=3.1701 @ step 1650 (108M tok, barely over half the budget), final degrades to 3.2789 (+0.1088) while train_loss keeps falling to 2.285. The real headline finding of this wave: at a FIXED, small, repeated token budget, bigger models overfit faster, so naive fixed-token scaling-law comparisons need early-stopping (or a fixed-epoch, not fixed-step, budget) to stay fair -- ties directly to the project's existing Muennighoff-ceiling concept (RW-1/D-015). See D-045.

variable_changed: milestone_steps added (early/mid/final snapshots), micro_batch=64 (cloud sweet spot) instead of 16 -- otherwise identical recipe, same seed
verdict: Reproduces the original baseline within the D-035 noise floor (3.4954 vs 3.5037, delta 0.0083) despite a different micro_batch/grad_accum factorization (D-040) -- captured early/mid/final milestone checkpoints for notebooks/08_eval_deep_dive.ipynb (phase 6, D-046).
